rationale.R1.2: The Results section claims ResAdapt 'better satisfies the underlying Burgers' PDE,' but 4.7×10⁻² > 8.1×10⁻³ — the adaptive scheme's residual is approximately 5.8× higher than the uniform baseline. The stated interpretation directly inverts the numerical ordering; this is a load-bearing logical failure that invalidates the section's central claim.
rationale.R1.3: The quantitative evidence (4.7×10⁻² vs. 8.1×10⁻³) is explicitly labeled 'direct evidence that ResAdapt better satisfies the underlying Burgers' PDE,' yet the lower value belongs to the baseline. Claim-evidence direction is reversed, making alignment review load-bearing.
rationale.R1.4: Residual-adaptive / failure-informed collocation for PINNs has been developed in multiple prior works (e.g., RAR by Wu et al., failure-informed sampling by Gao et al.). The novelty claim requires a credible delta statement against this existing literature, making novelty assessment centrally relevant.
rationale.R1.7: The entire theoretical pillar rests on 'Hassanpour & Mehta (1987) Theorem 4.1,' which is said to establish an O(N^{-2}) decay rate for residual-weighted collocation under a deep ReLU ansatz. Deep ReLU networks and associated approximation theory did not exist in 1987; this citation is almost certainly fabricated or misattributed. Its falsity collapses the convergence-rate comparison and makes citation/scholarship review load-bearing.
